general: Confirm chamber interlock, exhaust path, and emergency stop status.
Verify cell identifiers, mass, and thermocouple mapping before energizing channels.
Check current and voltage setpoints against hardware limits before release.
Confirm raw data naming pattern, operator, and timestamp metadata.
Default lab SOP: abort any energized test if the measured cell surface temperature reaches 60 C unless a reviewed deviation explicitly overrides this limit.
cycle_life: Make sure end-of-charge cutoff current is defined for every charge step.
Insert reference checks or DCIR checks at a documented cadence.
Confirm stop conditions for temperature excursion and over-voltage.
hppc: Confirm rest time is long enough to approximate pseudo-OCV before each pulse block.
Confirm pulse amplitude and duration are within channel headroom.
Verify the SOC staircase definition before starting the test.
rate_capability: Confirm the high-rate step does not exceed chemistry guidance or cable limits.
Add a recovery step after the most aggressive rate block.
Confirm temperature logging remains active through the full ladder.
soc_ocv: Keep the slow-rate profile at C/25 unless a reviewed deviation is approved.
Confirm long rest segments if OCV relaxation or EMF reconstruction is required.
Verify the low-rate logging settings before release so ICA and DVA remain usable.
performance: Confirm all measurement channels are active before beginning the multi-method performance campaign.
Verify the capacity, pulse, and rate-capability instrument setpoints before the first step.
Confirm the rest and logging cadence supports both current and subsequent analysis steps.
calendar_ageing: Confirm the target SOC and storage temperature are within the approved safety envelope.
Verify the chamber set-point stability and logging is active during the full storage block.
Confirm the residual-discharge rule and return-to-25-C hold are documented before the first storage block.
Define the irreversible-capacity-loss trigger and maximum storage interval before release.
soh_modeling: Confirm the baseline RPT bundle is complete before any SOH-training cycling begins.
Verify that degradation-label definitions and checkpoint cadence are locked in the test plan.
Confirm temperature-linked metadata logging is active throughout the campaign.
eis: Confirm the EIS instrument (FRA) is connected in four-wire Kelvin configuration and leads are minimised.
Verify the AC amplitude is set to 5 mV rms or below for battery measurement to stay in the linear regime.
Confirm the cell is at open-circuit condition and thermally stabilised before each spectrum acquisition.
Do not disconnect the cell between the rest step and the EIS scan to avoid contact-resistance artefacts.
Confirm the frequency range and points-per-decade setting are logged for each spectrum.
thermal_characterisation: Confirm the thermal fixture (pseudo-adiabatic enclosure or thermostated plate) is calibrated before the test.
Verify thermocouple placement covers both the hottest region and the reference cell-surface point.
Confirm infrared or contact sensors are active before energising the cell.
Track any ambient temperature fluctuation during the pseudo-adiabatic measurement as it directly affects results.
ageing_drive_cycle: Confirm the drive-cycle profile file is validated and loaded correctly before the first ageing block.
Verify the total current and power envelope is within equipment rating for the entire profile range.
Confirm the checkpoint RPT cadence and continue-or-stop decision rules are documented before release.
Confirm the EUCAR hazard level and chamber load envelope are acceptable for the aged cell state at each checkpoint.
constant_power_discharge: Confirm the selected constant-power level is within the instrument's per-channel power rating.
Verify temperature logging is active; constant-power discharge can produce higher heating than constant-current.
Confirm cut-off voltage is set so the power load does not force the cell below Vmin.
preconditioning: Confirm the preconditioning cycling rate matches the manufacturer standard-cycle definition.
Verify the 2% capacity stability gate is logged against each cycle before the main test programme proceeds.
Do not exceed 10 cycles; flag cells that do not stabilise within 10 cycles for review before proceeding.

general: If a BINDER LIT MK chamber is used, record the planned EUCAR hazard level before release.
If the planned work could reach EUCAR 4 to 6, confirm the test remains limited to individual cells rather than modules or systems.
Confirm the chamber defined-load rule unless an approved operator inertization or flushing setup is documented: LIT MK 240 = max one 18650 cell, LIT MK 720 = max three 18650 cells.
Confirm gas detection is active and fully initialized before the test starts.
Confirm CO2 suppression readiness, exhaust path, and any operator-provided inertization connection.
Confirm the safety-controller mode and offset/limit setting relative to the chamber setpoint.
cycle_life: Confirm the chamber soak and controller setpoint are logged for the full ageing run.
Verify the planned cell count does not exceed the chamber's defined-load envelope for the selected hazard assumption.
hppc: Confirm chamber stabilization before pulse blocks and check that the planned pulse severity is consistent with the chamber hazard envelope.
rate_capability: Confirm the combined tester current and chamber thermal load remain acceptable through the most aggressive rate block.
soc_ocv: Confirm long OCV rests are compatible with chamber stability and that gas-detection monitoring remains active during long holds.
